item.description:
Fixes

  • Sidebar no longer disappears — exiting fullscreen in the same window (or quitting a fullscreen app) previously left the strip hidden until you switched windows; it now returns within 2 seconds. It also correctly stays hidden over fullscreen games and video apps
  • Actions always hit the window you selected — switching tabs, filtering, or clicking a just-closed window can no longer focus, close, or minimize a different window; impossible targets now fail with a clear message instead
  • Rapid switching is safe — quickly clicking several windows in a row no longer lets an earlier switch "steal back" focus mid-animation
  • Multi-display fullscreen — exiting fullscreen to reach a window now targets the correct display, and windows no longer jump to the main screen
  • Cross-Space browsing without Screen Recording — windows on other Spaces appear even when the permission is declined

Improvements

  • VoiceOver can now navigate and activate all window cards
  • One permission prompt at launch instead of three; Screen Recording is requested when first needed
  • Panel gets a minimum size, ⌘K focuses search, and the Recent tab fits its content
  • Faster first open; screenshot cache is now memory-bounded
  • Pinned windows survive app renames and language changes
  • Clearer window-state indicators and slightly larger card labels

Fixes

  • Sidebar cards are now clickable on every Mac — clicks on the strip were silently swallowed on some machines because the sidebar never takes keyboard focus; cards now respond to the first click reliably
  • Clean rounded corners — the blur material no longer bleeds past the rounded corners as faint square lines (most visible against light backgrounds), in both the expanded strip and the collapsed edge handle
  • The same corner fix is applied to the main panel, the carousel, and toast messages for a consistent look
  • A hesitant drag-to-pin released early no longer switches windows by accident — short drags stay inert, exactly like a cancelled gesture

New: Sidebar Mode (optional)

A persistent work strip on the right edge of your screen — see your recent windows at a glance and switch with one click, no hotkey needed.

  • Off by default — enable it from the menu bar: Show Sidebar
  • Pin your long-lived windows (right-click or drag a card up) — they keep a fixed position, so switching becomes muscle memory
  • Recent windows fill the rest, up to 8 cards, adapting to your screen height
  • Collapse to a slim edge handle — hover to peek, click a card to switch; the strip never steals focus
  • Hides automatically over full-screen apps
